summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates host info and runs shell commands at install time
details: This package impersonates the official @solana/web3.js library under the @solana-labs/* scope. Its package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs automatically on npm install. install.js reads host identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), process.platform), probes the filesystem (fs.existsSync on multiple paths), POSTs data over https, and invokes execSync with curl and powershell commands. The combination of a typosquatted scope name, automatic install-time execution, system-information collection, outbound HTTPS POST, and shell-out via curl/powershell is the canonical install-time credential/host exfiltration and dropper pattern. Installing this package will run attacker-controlled code on the developer or build machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
